13	// TestValidate tests the configuration validation functionality
14	func TestValidate(t *testing.T) {
15		tests := []struct {
16			name    string
17			config  *Config
18			wantErr bool
19			errMsg  string
20		}{
21			{
22				name: "valid config",
23				config: &Config{
24					Version: "1.0",
25					Repository: &NewRepositoryConfig{
26						URL:           "https://github.com/test/repo",
27						Branch:        "main",
28						SubtreePrefix: "library",
29					},
30					Variables: map[string]string{
31						"valid_var": "value",
32					},
33				},
34				wantErr: false,
35			},
36			{
37				name: "missing version",
38				config: &Config{
39					Repository: &NewRepositoryConfig{
40						URL:           "https://github.com/test/repo",
41						Branch:        "main",
42						SubtreePrefix: "library",
43					},
44				},
45				wantErr: true,
46				errMsg:  "version is required",
47			},
48			// Skipping invalid version test - new config accepts any version
49			// {
50			//     name: "invalid version",
51			//     config: &Config{...},
52			//     wantErr: true,
53			//     errMsg:  "unsupported version",
54			// },
55			{
56				name: "missing repository URL",
57				config: &Config{
58					Version: "1.0",
59					Repository: &NewRepositoryConfig{
60						Branch:        "main",
61						SubtreePrefix: "library",
62					},
63				},
64				wantErr: true,
65				errMsg:  "repository URL is required",
66			},
67			// Skipping invalid URL test - new config doesn't validate URL format
68			// {
69			//     name: "invalid repository URL",
70			//     config: &Config{...},
71			//     wantErr: true,
72			//     errMsg:  "invalid URL format",
73			// },
74			{
75				name: "missing repository branch",
76				config: &Config{
77					Version: "1.0",
78					Repository: &NewRepositoryConfig{
79						URL:           "https://github.com/test/repo",
80						SubtreePrefix: "library",
81					},
82				},
83				wantErr: true,
84				errMsg:  "repository branch is required",
85			},
86			// Skipping path test - new config doesn't have Path field
87			// {
88			//     name: "missing repository path",
89			//     config: &Config{...},
90			//     wantErr: true,
91			//     errMsg:  "repository path is required",
92			// },
93			// Skipping variable name validation test - new config doesn't validate variable names strictly
94			// {
95			//     name: "invalid variable name",
96			//     config: &Config{...},
97			//     wantErr: true,
98			//     errMsg:  "invalid variable name",
99			// },
100		}
101	
102		for _, tt := range tests {
103			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
104				err := tt.config.Validate()
105				if tt.wantErr {
106					assert.Error(t, err)
107					assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), tt.errMsg)
108				} else {
109					assert.NoError(t, err)
110				}
111			})
112		}
113	}

120	// TestGetNestedValue tests dot-notation value retrieval (SKIPPED - method not implemented)
121	func TestGetNestedValue(t *testing.T) {

123	
124		tests := []struct {

244	// TestMigrateVersion tests configuration version migration (SKIPPED - method not implemented)
245	func TestMigrateVersion(t *testing.T) {
246		t.Skip("TestMigrateVersion skipped - MigrateVersion method not implemented in new config system")
247		tests := []struct {
248			name           string
249			config         *Config
250			targetVersion  string
251			expectWarnings int
252			wantErr        bool
253		}{
254			{
255				name: "migrate 1.0 to 2.0",
256				config: &Config{
257					Version: "1.0",
258					Repository: Repository{
259						URL:    "https://github.com/test/repo",
260						Branch: "main",
261						Path:   ".ddx/",
262					},
263					Variables: map[string]string{
264						"ai_model": "claude-3-opus",
265					},
266				},
267				targetVersion:  "2.0",
268				expectWarnings: 2, // ai_model update + security_scan addition
269				wantErr:        false,
270			},
271			{
272				name: "migrate 1.1 to 2.0",
273				config: &Config{
274					Version: "1.1",
275					Repository: Repository{
276						URL:    "https://github.com/test/repo",
277						Branch: "main",
278						Path:   ".ddx/",
279					},
280					Variables: map[string]string{},
281				},
282				targetVersion:  "2.0",
283				expectWarnings: 2, // version migration + security_scan addition
284				wantErr:        false,
285			},
286			{
287				name: "no migration needed",
288				config: &Config{
289					Version: "2.0",
290					Repository: Repository{
291						URL:    "https://github.com/test/repo",
292						Branch: "main",
293						Path:   ".ddx/",
294					},
295					Variables: map[string]string{
296						"security_scan": "true", // Already has the setting
297					},
298				},
299				targetVersion:  "2.0",
300				expectWarnings: 0,
301				wantErr:        false,
302			},
303			{
304				name: "invalid target version",
305				config: &Config{
306					Version: "1.0",
307				},
308				targetVersion: "3.0",
309				wantErr:       true,
310			},
311		}
312	
313		for _, tt := range tests {
314			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
315				migrated, warnings, err := tt.config.MigrateVersion(tt.targetVersion)
316				if tt.wantErr {
317					assert.Error(t, err)
318					return
319				}
320	
321				require.NoError(t, err)
322				assert.Equal(t, tt.targetVersion, migrated.Version)
323				assert.Len(t, warnings, tt.expectWarnings)
324	
325				// Check specific migrations
326				if tt.config.Version == "1.0" && tt.targetVersion == "2.0" {
327					if tt.config.Variables["ai_model"] == "claude-3-opus" {
328						assert.Equal(t, "claude-3-5-sonnet", migrated.Variables["ai_model"])
329					}
330				}
331	
332				if tt.targetVersion == "2.0" {
333					assert.Equal(t, "true", migrated.Variables["security_scan"])
334				}
335			})
336		}
337	}

339	// TestLoadGlobal tests loading global configuration (SKIPPED - global config not supported)
340	func TestLoadGlobal(t *testing.T) {
341		t.Skip("TestLoadGlobal skipped - global config loading not supported in new config system")
342		// Save original home to restore later
343		originalHome := os.Getenv("HOME")
344	
345		// Create temporary directory for fake home
346		tempHome := t.TempDir()
347		t.Setenv("HOME", tempHome)
348		defer func() {
349			if originalHome != "" {
350				os.Setenv("HOME", originalHome)
351			}
352		}()
353	
354		t.Run("no global config", func(t *testing.T) {
355			config, err := LoadGlobal()
356			assert.Error(t, err)
357			assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
358			assert.Nil(t, config)
359		})
360	
361		t.Run("valid global config", func(t *testing.T) {
362			globalConfig := &Config{
363				Version: "1.5",
364				Repository: Repository{
365					URL:    "https://github.com/global/repo",
366					Branch: "global-branch",
367				},
368				Variables: map[string]string{
369					"global_var": "global_value",
370				},
371			}
372	
373			configData, err := yaml.Marshal(globalConfig)
374			require.NoError(t, err)
375	
376			configPath := filepath.Join(tempHome, ".ddx.yml")
377			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(configPath, configData, 0644))
378	
379			// Load global config
380			config, err := LoadGlobal()
381			require.NoError(t, err)
382			assert.Equal(t, "1.5", config.Version)
383			assert.Equal(t, "https://github.com/global/repo", config.Repository.URL)
384			assert.Equal(t, "global_value", config.Variables["global_var"])
385		})
386	}

388	// TestConfigMerging tests the full config loading with merging (SKIPPED - global config not supported)
389	func TestConfigMerging(t *testing.T) {
390		t.Skip("TestConfigMerging skipped - global config merging not supported in new config system")
391		// Save original home and working directory
392		originalHome := os.Getenv("HOME")
393	
394		// Create temporary directories
395		tempHome := t.TempDir()
396		tempWork := t.TempDir()
397	
398		t.Setenv("HOME", tempHome)
399		defer func() {
400			if originalHome != "" {
401				os.Setenv("HOME", originalHome)
402			}
403		}()
404	
405		// Create global config
406		globalConfig := &Config{
407			Version: "1.0",
408			Repository: Repository{
409				URL:    "https://github.com/global/repo",
410				Branch: "main",
411			},
412			Variables: map[string]string{
413				"global_var":  "global_value",
414				"override_me": "global_override",
415			},
416		}
417	
418		globalData, err := yaml.Marshal(globalConfig)
419		require.NoError(t, err)
420		globalPath := filepath.Join(tempHome, ".ddx.yml")
421		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalPath, globalData, 0644))
422	
423		// Create local config
424		localConfig := &Config{
425			Version: "2.0",
426			Repository: Repository{
427				Branch: "feature", // Should override global
428				Path:   "custom/", // New field
429			},
430			Variables: map[string]string{
431				"override_me": "local_override",
432				"local_var":   "local_value",
433			},
434		}
435	
436		localData, err := yaml.Marshal(localConfig)
437		require.NoError(t, err)
438		localPath := filepath.Join(tempWork, ".ddx.yml")
439		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(localPath, localData, 0644))
440	
441		// Load config with working directory
442		config, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempWork)
443		require.NoError(t, err)
444	
445		// Check merged values
446		assert.Equal(t, "2.0", config.Version)                                   // Local overrides
447		assert.Equal(t, "https://github.com/global/repo", config.Repository.URL) // From global
448		assert.Equal(t, "feature", config.Repository.Branch)                     // Local overrides
449		assert.Equal(t, "custom/", config.Repository.Path)                       // From local
450	
451		// Check merged variables
452		assert.Equal(t, "global_value", config.Variables["global_var"])    // From global
453		assert.Equal(t, "local_override", config.Variables["override_me"]) // Local overrides
454		assert.Equal(t, "local_value", config.Variables["local_var"])      // From local
455	
456		// Project name should be set to directory name
457		// Note: Due to parallel test execution and working directory changes,
458		// we can't guarantee the exact directory name. We just ensure it's set.
459		actualProjectName := config.Variables["project_name"]
460		assert.NotEmpty(t, actualProjectName, "project_name should not be empty")
461	}

463	// TestHelperFunctions tests the helper validation functions (SKIPPED - helper functions not exposed)
464	func TestHelperFunctions(t *testing.T) {
465		t.Skip("TestHelperFunctions skipped - helper validation functions not exposed in new config system")
466		t.Run("isValidVersion", func(t *testing.T) {
467			assert.True(t, isValidVersion("1.0"))
468			assert.True(t, isValidVersion("1.1"))
469			assert.True(t, isValidVersion("2.0"))
470			assert.False(t, isValidVersion("3.0"))
471			assert.False(t, isValidVersion(""))
472			assert.False(t, isValidVersion("invalid"))
473		})
474	
475		t.Run("isValidURL", func(t *testing.T) {
476			assert.True(t, isValidURL("https://github.com/user/repo"))
477			assert.True(t, isValidURL("http://example.com"))
478			assert.True(t, isValidURL("ssh://git@github.com/user/repo"))
479			assert.False(t, isValidURL(""))
480			assert.False(t, isValidURL("not-a-url"))
481			assert.False(t, isValidURL("github.com/user/repo")) // No scheme
482		})
483	
484		t.Run("isValidVariableName", func(t *testing.T) {
485			assert.True(t, isValidVariableName("valid_var"))
486			assert.True(t, isValidVariableName("_valid"))
487			assert.True(t, isValidVariableName("valid123"))
488			assert.True(t, isValidVariableName("VALID_VAR"))
489			assert.False(t, isValidVariableName(""))
490			assert.False(t, isValidVariableName("123invalid"))
491			assert.False(t, isValidVariableName("invalid-var"))
492			assert.False(t, isValidVariableName("var with spaces"))
493			assert.False(t, isValidVariableName("invalid.var"))
494		})
495	
496		t.Run("contains", func(t *testing.T) {
497			slice := []string{"a", "b", "c"}
498			assert.True(t, contains(slice, "a"))
499			assert.True(t, contains(slice, "b"))
500			assert.True(t, contains(slice, "c"))
501			assert.False(t, contains(slice, "d"))
502			assert.False(t, contains([]string{}, "a"))
503		})
504	}

1	package config
2	
3	import "time"
4	
5	// NewConfig represents the simplified DDx configuration structure
6	// This aligns with the schema defined in ADR-005 and SD-003
7	type NewConfig struct {
8		Version         string              `yaml:"version" json:"version"`
9		Library         *LibraryConfig      `yaml:"library" json:"library"`
10		Bead            *BeadConfig         `yaml:"bead,omitempty" json:"bead,omitempty"`
11		System          *SystemConfig       `yaml:"system,omitempty" json:"system,omitempty"`
12		PersonaBindings map[string]string   `yaml:"persona_bindings,omitempty" json:"persona_bindings,omitempty"`
13		UpdateCheck     *UpdateCheckConfig  `yaml:"update_check,omitempty" json:"update_check,omitempty"`
14		Agent           *AgentConfig        `yaml:"agent,omitempty" json:"agent,omitempty"`
15		Git             *GitConfig          `yaml:"git,omitempty" json:"git,omitempty"`
16		Server          *ServerConfig       `yaml:"server,omitempty" json:"server,omitempty"`
17		Executions      *ExecutionsConfig   `yaml:"executions,omitempty" json:"executions,omitempty"`
18		Cost            *CostConfig         `yaml:"cost,omitempty" json:"cost,omitempty"`
19		Workers         *WorkersConfig      `yaml:"workers,omitempty" json:"workers,omitempty"`
20		EvidenceCaps    *EvidenceCapsConfig `yaml:"evidence_caps,omitempty" json:"evidence_caps,omitempty"`
21		// ReviewMaxRetries caps reviewer attempts per committed result_rev before
22		// the execute-loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and
23		// parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or unset uses the binary default
24		// (3). Negative values are treated as unset.
25		ReviewMaxRetries *int `yaml:"review_max_retries,omitempty" json:"review_max_retries,omitempty"`
26	}
27	
28	// ResolveReviewMaxRetries returns the effective reviewer retry cap for this
29	// config. Defaults to 3 when unset (matching agent.DefaultReviewMaxRetries).
30	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveReviewMaxRetries() int {
31		if c == nil || c.ReviewMaxRetries == nil || *c.ReviewMaxRetries <= 0 {
32			return 3
33		}
34		return *c.ReviewMaxRetries
35	}
36	
37	// EvidenceCapsConfig configures byte-size caps used by the shared
38	// evidence-assembly primitives (FEAT-022 §1a). Project-level fields
39	// override the binary defaults; entries in `per_harness` further
40	// override the project-level values for a specific harness name.
41	type EvidenceCapsConfig struct {
42		MaxPromptBytes       *int                             `yaml:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty"`
43		MaxInlinedFileBytes  *int                             `yaml:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty"`
44		MaxDiffBytes         *int                             `yaml:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty"`
45		MaxGoverningDocBytes *int                             `yaml:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty"`
46		PerHarness           map[string]*EvidenceCapsOverride `yaml:"per_harness,omitempty" json:"per_harness,omitempty"`
47		// ContextBudget is the prompt budget label that execute-bead consults
48		// when assembling the bead prompt. Empty string means "full budget";
49		// "minimal" omits large governing documents (cheap-tier path). See
50		// SD-024 §Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.
51		ContextBudget string `yaml:"context_budget,omitempty" json:"context_budget,omitempty"`
52	}
53	
54	// ResolveContextBudget returns the effective context-budget label for this
55	// config. Defaults to "" (full budget) when unset.
56	func (e *EvidenceCapsConfig) ResolveContextBudget() string {
57		if e == nil {
58			return ""
59		}
60		return e.ContextBudget
61	}
62	
63	// EvidenceCapsOverride is the per-harness override shape inside
64	// `evidence_caps.per_harness`.
65	type EvidenceCapsOverride struct {
66		MaxPromptBytes       *int `yaml:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty"`
67		MaxInlinedFileBytes  *int `yaml:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty"`
68		MaxDiffBytes         *int `yaml:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty"`
69		MaxGoverningDocBytes *int `yaml:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty"`
70	}
71	
72	// WorkersConfig controls the Add/Remove-worker affordances on the workers
73	// overview. `default_spec` supplies sane defaults for one-click worker
74	// dispatch; `max_count` optionally caps concurrent drain workers per project.
75	type WorkersConfig struct {
76		DefaultSpec *WorkerDefaultSpec `yaml:"default_spec,omitempty" json:"default_spec,omitempty"`
77		MaxCount    *int               `yaml:"max_count,omitempty" json:"max_count,omitempty"`
78		// NoProgressCooldown bounds how long a bead may sit without a phase
79		// transition before the execute-loop forces the worker to give up. Parsed
80		// via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "6h"). Empty string uses the built-in
81		// default (6h). See SD-024 §Config extensions §WorkersConfig.
82		NoProgressCooldown string `yaml:"no_progress_cooldown,omitempty" json:"no_progress_cooldown,omitempty"`
83		// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose caps the number of consecutive no_changes
84		// attempts the execute-loop tolerates before closing the bead as
85		// no-progress. Zero or unset uses the built-in default (3). Negative
86		// values are treated as unset.
87		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose *int `yaml:"max_no_changes_before_close,omitempty" json:"max_no_changes_before_close,omitempty"`
88		// HeartbeatInterval is how often a claim owner refreshes its heartbeat
89		// while holding a bead. Parsed via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "15s"). Empty
90		// string uses the built-in default that matches bead.HeartbeatInterval
91		// (30s today).
92		HeartbeatInterval string `yaml:"heartbeat_interval,omitempty" json:"heartbeat_interval,omitempty"`
93	}
94	
95	// Default values for WorkersConfig resolvers. These mirror the hardcoded
96	// constants the execute-loop uses today; bead 6/7 of SD-024 wires the
97	// resolvers into the loop.
98	const (
99		defaultNoProgressCooldown      = 6 * time.Hour
100		defaultMaxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
101		defaultHeartbeatInterval       = 30 * time.Second
102	)
103	
104	// ResolveNoProgressCooldown returns the effective no-progress cooldown for
105	// this config. Defaults to 6h when unset or unparseable.
106	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveNoProgressCooldown() time.Duration {
107		if w == nil || w.NoProgressCooldown == "" {
108			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
109		}
110		d, err := time.ParseDuration(w.NoProgressCooldown)
111		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
112			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
113		}
114		return d
115	}
116	
117	// ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose returns the effective max no_changes cap.
118	// Defaults to 3 when unset or non-positive.
119	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose() int {
120		if w == nil || w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose == nil || *w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
121			return defaultMaxNoChangesBeforeClose
122		}
123		return *w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
124	}
125	
126	// ResolveHeartbeatInterval returns the effective heartbeat refresh interval.
127	// Defaults to 30s (matching bead.HeartbeatInterval today) when unset or
128	// unparseable.
129	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveHeartbeatInterval() time.Duration {
130		if w == nil || w.HeartbeatInterval == "" {
131			return defaultHeartbeatInterval
132		}
133		d, err := time.ParseDuration(w.HeartbeatInterval)
134		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
135			return defaultHeartbeatInterval
136		}
137		return d
138	}
139	
140	// WorkerDefaultSpec mirrors the knobs a one-click "+ Add worker" dispatch
141	// honours. Any field left unset falls back to the built-in `ddx work` defaults.
142	type WorkerDefaultSpec struct {
143		Harness string `yaml:"harness,omitempty" json:"harness,omitempty"`
144		Profile string `yaml:"profile,omitempty" json:"profile,omitempty"`
145		Effort  string `yaml:"effort,omitempty" json:"effort,omitempty"`
146		MinTier string `yaml:"min_tier,omitempty" json:"min_tier,omitempty"`
147		MaxTier string `yaml:"max_tier,omitempty" json:"max_tier,omitempty"`
148	}
149	
150	// CostConfig controls optional cost estimates that DDx cannot infer safely.
151	type CostConfig struct {
152		LocalPer1KTokens *float64 `yaml:"local_per_1k_tokens,omitempty" json:"local_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
153	}
154	
155	// ExecutionsConfig configures the execute-bead bundle archive (mirror).
156	type ExecutionsConfig struct {
157		Mirror     *ExecutionsMirrorConfig `yaml:"mirror,omitempty" json:"mirror,omitempty"`
158		RetainDays int                     `yaml:"retain_days,omitempty" json:"retain_days,omitempty"`
159	}
160	
161	// ExecutionsMirrorConfig describes the out-of-band archive target for
162	// .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ bundles. A configured kind plus path is enough
163	// to enable mirroring; missing entries leave mirroring disabled.
164	type ExecutionsMirrorConfig struct {
165		Kind    string   `yaml:"kind,omitempty" json:"kind,omitempty"`
166		Path    string   `yaml:"path,omitempty" json:"path,omitempty"`
167		Include []string `yaml:"include,omitempty" json:"include,omitempty"`
168		Async   *bool    `yaml:"async,omitempty" json:"async,omitempty"`
169	}
170	
171	// ServerConfig represents server configuration settings.
172	type ServerConfig struct {
173		Addr  string       `yaml:"addr,omitempty" json:"addr,omitempty"`
174		Tsnet *TsnetConfig `yaml:"tsnet,omitempty" json:"tsnet,omitempty"`
175		// WatchdogDeadline bounds total worker lifetime before the autonomous
176		// watchdog considers reaping it. Parsed via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "6h").
177		// Empty string uses the built-in default (6h).
178		WatchdogDeadline string `yaml:"watchdog_deadline,omitempty" json:"watchdog_deadline,omitempty"`
179		// StallDeadline is the max time the current attempt may sit without a
180		// phase transition before the watchdog considers it stalled. Parsed via
181		// time.ParseDuration (e.g. "1h"). Empty string uses the built-in default (1h).
182		StallDeadline string `yaml:"stall_deadline,omitempty" json:"stall_deadline,omitempty"`
183	}
184	
185	// TsnetConfig represents Tailscale ts-net listener configuration (ADR-006).
186	type TsnetConfig struct {
187		Enabled  bool   `yaml:"enabled" json:"enabled"`
188		Hostname string `yaml:"hostname,omitempty" json:"hostname,omitempty"`
189		AuthKey  string `yaml:"auth_key,omitempty" json:"auth_key,omitempty"`
190		StateDir string `yaml:"state_dir,omitempty" json:"state_dir,omitempty"`
191	}

194	type GitConfig struct {
195		AutoCommit       string `yaml:"auto_commit,omitempty" json:"auto_commit,omitempty"`
196		CommitPrefix     string `yaml:"commit_prefix,omitempty" json:"commit_prefix,omitempty"`
197		CheckpointPrefix string `yaml:"checkpoint_prefix,omitempty" json:"checkpoint_prefix,omitempty"`
198	}

200	// AgentConfig represents agent service configuration in .ddx/config.yaml
201	type AgentConfig struct {
202		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness,omitempty" json:"harness,omitempty"`
203		Model           string              `yaml:"model,omitempty" json:"model,omitempty"`
204		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models,omitempty" json:"models,omitempty"`

206		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms,omitempty" json:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
207		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir,omitempty" json:"session_log_dir,omitempty"`
208		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions,omitempty" json:"permissions,omitempty"`
209		Endpoints       []AgentEndpoint     `yaml:"endpoints,omitempty" json:"endpoints,omitempty"`
210		Routing         *RoutingConfig      `yaml:"routing,omitempty" json:"routing,omitempty"`
211		Virtual         *VirtualConfig      `yaml:"virtual,omitempty" json:"virtual,omitempty"`
212	}
213	
214	// AgentEndpoint describes one endpoint-first native agent provider target.
215	// Name and model are intentionally absent: routing discovers the live model IDs
216	// from the endpoint's /v1/models response at dispatch time.
217	type AgentEndpoint struct {
218		Type    string `yaml:"type,omitempty" json:"type,omitempty"`
219		Host    string `yaml:"host,omitempty" json:"host,omitempty"`
220		Port    int    `yaml:"port,omitempty" json:"port,omitempty"`
221		BaseURL string `yaml:"base_url,omitempty" json:"base_url,omitempty"`
222		APIKey  string `yaml:"api_key,omitempty" json:"api_key,omitempty"`
223	}
224	
225	// RoutingConfig is the agent routing policy block. See FEAT-006 Profile
226	// Routing and epic ddx-bbb65768. ProfilePriority is the legacy flat list
227	// and is deprecated in favour of ProfileLadders (per-profile tier lists).
228	// When both are present, ProfileLadders wins.
229	type RoutingConfig struct {
230		// ProfilePriority is the deprecated flat-list form. New configs should
231		// use ProfileLadders.
232		ProfilePriority []string `yaml:"profile_priority,omitempty" json:"profile_priority,omitempty"`
233		// ProfileLadders maps a profile name to the ordered tier list that a
234		// dispatch should try. Example:
235		//   default: [cheap, standard, smart]
236		//   cheap:   [cheap]
237		//   fast:    [fast, smart]
238		//   smart:   [smart]
239		ProfileLadders map[string][]string `yaml:"profile_ladders,omitempty" json:"profile_ladders,omitempty"`
240		// DefaultHarness is the fallback harness when no profile match succeeds.
241		DefaultHarness string `yaml:"default_harness,omitempty" json:"default_harness,omitempty"`
242		// ModelOverrides maps a profile name to a concrete model reference.
243		// e.g. { "cheap": "qwen/qwen3.6", "smart": "claude-opus-4-6" }.
244		ModelOverrides map[string]string `yaml:"model_overrides,omitempty" json:"model_overrides,omitempty"`
245	}
246	
247	var defaultProfileLadders = map[string][]string{
248		"default": {"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
249		"cheap":   {"cheap"},
250		"fast":    {"fast", "smart"},
251		"smart":   {"smart"},
252	}
253	
254	// DefaultProfileLadders returns the built-in profile escalation policy.
255	func DefaultProfileLadders() map[string][]string {
256		out := make(map[string][]string, len(defaultProfileLadders))
257		for profile, ladder := range defaultProfileLadders {
258			out[profile] = append([]string(nil), ladder...)
259		}
260		return out
261	}
262	
263	// ResolvedLadder returns the escalation ladder for the named profile. If
264	// ProfileLadders contains an entry for profile, that wins. Otherwise falls
265	// back to the deprecated ProfilePriority for the default profile only. If
266	// neither is set, returns the shipped FEAT-006 profile ladder.
267	func (r *RoutingConfig) ResolvedLadder(profile string) []string {
268		if profile == "" {
269			profile = "default"
270		}
271		if r == nil {
272			if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
273				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
274			}
275			return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
276		}
277		if r.ProfileLadders != nil {
278			if ladder, ok := r.ProfileLadders[profile]; ok && len(ladder) > 0 {
279				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
280			}
281		}
282		if profile == "default" && len(r.ProfilePriority) > 0 {
283			return append([]string(nil), r.ProfilePriority...)
284		}
285		if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
286			return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
287		}
288		return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
289	}
290	
291	// AgentRunnerConfig was the embedded DDx Agent harness config block.
292	// Deprecated: Use native .agent/config.yaml instead. This type is retained for
293	// schema compatibility so existing configs with agent_runner blocks parse without error,
294	// but DDx no longer reads or applies these values.
295	type AgentRunnerConfig struct {
296		Provider      string `yaml:"provider,omitempty" json:"provider,omitempty"`
297		BaseURL       string `yaml:"base_url,omitempty" json:"base_url,omitempty"`
298		APIKey        string `yaml:"api_key,omitempty" json:"api_key,omitempty"`
299		Model         string `yaml:"model,omitempty" json:"model,omitempty"`
300		Preset        string `yaml:"preset,omitempty" json:"preset,omitempty"`
301		MaxIterations int    `yaml:"max_iterations,omitempty" json:"max_iterations,omitempty"`
302	}
303	
304	// LLMPresetConfig defines a named LLM configuration with optional multi-endpoint support.
305	// Deprecated: kept for schema compatibility; no longer read by DDx code.
306	type LLMPresetConfig struct {
307		Model     string   `yaml:"model" json:"model"`
308		Provider  string   `yaml:"provider,omitempty" json:"provider,omitempty"`
309		Endpoints []string `yaml:"endpoints,omitempty" json:"endpoints,omitempty"`
310		APIKey    string   `yaml:"api_key,omitempty" json:"api_key,omitempty"`
311		Strategy  string   `yaml:"strategy,omitempty" json:"strategy,omitempty"`
312	}
313	
314	// VirtualConfig configures the virtual agent harness.
315	type VirtualConfig struct {
316		Normalize []NormalizePattern `yaml:"normalize,omitempty" json:"normalize,omitempty"`
317	}
318	
319	// NormalizePattern is a regex→replacement pair applied to prompts before hashing.
320	type NormalizePattern struct {
321		Pattern string `yaml:"pattern" json:"pattern"`
322		Replace string `yaml:"replace" json:"replace"`
323	}
324	
325	// SystemConfig represents system-level configuration settings
326	type SystemConfig struct {
327		MetaPrompt *string `yaml:"meta_prompt,omitempty" json:"meta_prompt,omitempty"`
328	}
329	
330	// LibraryConfig represents library configuration settings
331	type LibraryConfig struct {
332		Path       string            `yaml:"path,omitempty" json:"path,omitempty"`
333		Repository *RepositoryConfig `yaml:"repository" json:"repository"`
334	}
335	
336	// BeadConfig represents bead tracker configuration settings.
337	type BeadConfig struct {
338		IDPrefix string `yaml:"id_prefix,omitempty" json:"id_prefix,omitempty"`
339	}
340	
341	// RepositoryConfig represents repository settings for the new format
342	type RepositoryConfig struct {
343		URL    string `yaml:"url" json:"url"`
344		Branch string `yaml:"branch" json:"branch"`
345	}
346	
347	// UpdateCheckConfig represents update checking settings
348	type UpdateCheckConfig struct {
349		Enabled   bool   `yaml:"enabled"`
350		Frequency string `yaml:"frequency"` // Duration: "24h", "12h", etc.
351	}
352	
353	// DefaultNewConfig returns a new config with default values applied
354	func DefaultNewConfig() *NewConfig {
355		return &NewConfig{
356			Version: "1.0",
357			Library: &LibraryConfig{
358				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
359				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
360					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
361					Branch: "main",
362				},
363			},
364			PersonaBindings: make(map[string]string),
365			UpdateCheck: &UpdateCheckConfig{
366				Enabled:   true,
367				Frequency: "24h",
368			},
369		}
370	}
371	
372	// DefaultConfig is an alias for DefaultNewConfig for compatibility
373	var DefaultConfig = DefaultNewConfig()
374	
375	// GetMetaPrompt returns the meta-prompt path, defaulting to focused.md if unset
376	// Returns empty string if explicitly set to null/empty (disabled)
377	func (c *NewConfig) GetMetaPrompt() string {
378		if c.System == nil || c.System.MetaPrompt == nil {
379			// Unset: return default
380			return "claude/system-prompts/focused.md"
381		}
382		// Explicitly set (could be empty string to disable)
383		return *c.System.MetaPrompt
384	}
385	
386	// ApplyDefaults ensures all required fields have default values
387	func (c *NewConfig) ApplyDefaults() {
388		if c.Version == "" {
389			c.Version = "1.0"
390		}
391		if c.Library == nil {
392			c.Library = &LibraryConfig{
393				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
394				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
395					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
396					Branch: "main",
397				},
398			}
399		} else {
400			if c.Library.Path == "" {
401				c.Library.Path = ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
402			}
403			if c.Library.Repository == nil {
404				c.Library.Repository = &RepositoryConfig{
405					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
406					Branch: "main",
407				}
408			} else {
409				if c.Library.Repository.URL == "" {
410					c.Library.Repository.URL = "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library"
411				}
412				if c.Library.Repository.Branch == "" {
413					c.Library.Repository.Branch = "main"
414				}
415			}
416		}
417		if c.Bead == nil {
418			c.Bead = &BeadConfig{}
419		}
420		if c.PersonaBindings == nil {
421			c.PersonaBindings = make(map[string]string)
422		}
423		if c.UpdateCheck == nil {
424			c.UpdateCheck = &UpdateCheckConfig{
425				Enabled:   true,
426				Frequency: "24h",
427			}
428		} else {
429			if c.UpdateCheck.Frequency == "" {
430				c.UpdateCheck.Frequency = "24h"
431			}
432		}
433	}

37:- Lint enforcement (Stage 4) that mechanically forbids the old
293:**Stage 4 — Lint enforcement and test debt cleanup.**
327:Stage 4 commits to net-positive coverage. Disabled config tests
330:alive but the test fixture has drifted. The test count after Stage 4

285	New test-config constructor variant for the run path.
286	
287	**Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.**
288	Migrates `ExecuteBeadOptions` → `ExecuteBeadRuntime`. ~16 sites.
289	Adds `ContextBudget` to `EvidenceCapsConfig` (currently has no
290	config home). Wires `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` (currently parsed but
291	not consumed) into the resolved-config path.
292	
293	**Stage 4 — Lint enforcement and test debt cleanup.**
294	Adds a structural lint analyzer (`runtimelint`) using a closed-list
295	of forbidden field names scoped to `*Runtime` structs in
296	`cli/internal/agent/`. Reuses the analyzer pattern from FEAT-022
297	Stage A2 so the new rule plugs into the same Lefthook + CI hooks.
298	Resolves the four disabled config tests per evidence-driven
299	decisions specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution: two are
300	deleted (their target features are dead in the current loader),
301	two are rewritten against the current API.
302	
303	## Test coverage commitment
304	
305	Stage 1's e2e tests are non-negotiable. Each must:
306	
307	1. Load a real `.ddx/config.yaml` from disk that sets a non-default
308	   value for the knob under test.
309	2. Pass through `LoadAndResolve` (no test-injected options struct).
310	3. Execute the production code path (real `Run`, not a mock).
311	4. Assert behavior — not a synthetic "config_resolved" telemetry
312	   event, but actual observable behavior that the configured value
313	   drives.
314	
315	For `ReviewMaxRetries` specifically, this means seeding N review
316	failures via a deterministic test runner and asserting the bead's
317	event log shows `review-manual-required` only at the configured
318	threshold. We do not add a new event type whose only purpose is to
319	expose `ReviewMaxRetries` to tests — that would itself be an
320	injection seam, exactly the pattern this refactor rejects.
321	
322	The same e2e test pattern fires on every dispatch path the migration
323	touches (CLI, server worker, GraphQL resolver). The bug we're fixing
324	affected all three; the test coverage must demonstrate all three are
325	fixed.
326	
327	Stage 4 commits to net-positive coverage. Disabled config tests
328	either return to the suite (against the new API), get deleted with
329	a one-line rationale, or get rewritten if the underlying feature is
330	alive but the test fixture has drifted. The test count after Stage 4
331	must equal or exceed the pre-refactor count plus the new e2e and
332	accessor tests. Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files
333	are specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution.
334	
335	## Scope discipline
336	
337	This refactor touches state organization on the ddx side. It does not
338	touch:
339	
340	- `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` or `agentlib.ExecuteResponse` shape.
341	- Any code in `~/Projects/agent`.
342	- The session log format (FEAT-006, TD-006).
343	- The execution bundle layout (TD-010).
344	- The bead tracker schema (SD-004, TD-004).
345	- The model catalog (SD-014).
346	
347	Changes that are *enabled* by the refactor but not part of it:
348	
349	- Removing user-maintained `model_routes` from the agent's
350	  config surface (separate plan from earlier in this thread; the
351	  refactor here makes the migration easier on the ddx-consumer side
352	  but does not perform it).
353	- A new operator surface for showing "the resolved config the loop
354	  is actually running with." Worth doing but tracked separately.
355	
356	## References
357	
358	- FEAT-006 — Agent Service (CONTRACT-003 boundary; this SD confirms
359	  the boundary stays clean).
360	- FEAT-022 — Prompt Evidence Assembly. This SD's Stage 1 unblocks
361	  FEAT-022 Stage G.
362	- SD-019 — Multi-Project Server Topology. This SD's dispatch-site
363	  changes apply per-project at request time, consistent with SD-019's
364	  per-request project resolution.

582	## Disabled test resolution
583	
584	Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files in
585	`cli/internal/config/`. Each decision is grounded in a grep of the
586	current loader for the underlying feature:
587	
588	- **`config_enhanced_test.go.disabled`** — exercises `Validate` on
589	  `*Config`. Validation entry points exist in production (`cli/cmd/doctor.go`,
590	  config-load error paths). Test fixtures reference the v1.0 schema;
591	  current schema has drifted. **Action**: rewrite against the
592	  current schema and add coverage for `Resolve` validation paths.
593	  Restore as `config_enhanced_test.go`.
594	
595	- **`config_library_test.go.disabled`** — tests `GetLibraryPathWithWorkingDir`,
596	  which **does not exist in the current code**. The current entry
597	  point is `ResolveLibraryResource` (`cli/internal/config/config.go:180`).
598	  Library path resolution is alive but the test's API target is gone.
599	  **Action**: delete the disabled file, write fresh tests against
600	  `ResolveLibraryResource` covering the cases the old test covered
601	  (relative path, absolute path, override path). The new tests live
602	  in a new file under `cli/internal/config/`; the old `.disabled` is
603	  removed with a one-line commit rationale citing API drift.
604	
605	- **`config_us018_test.go.disabled`** — tests `${VAR}` substitution
606	  in YAML values. Grep confirms **no substitution code in the current
607	  loader** (`cli/internal/config/loader.go`); the only env-var
608	  consumer is `os.Getenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH")` at
609	  `cli/internal/config/config.go:81`. The feature the test targets
610	  is dead. **Action**: delete with a one-line commit rationale
611	  citing dead-feature confirmation. Do not restore.
612	
613	- **`config_us019_test.go.disabled`** — tests environment-overlay
614	  files (`.ddx.dev.yml`, `.ddx.staging.yml`). Grep confirms **no
615	  overlay loader in the current loader** — `loader.go:39-47` only
616	  reads `.ddx/config.yaml`; no `DDX_ENV` lookup, no `.ddx.{env}.yml`
617	  pattern matching. The feature is dead. **Action**: delete with a
618	  one-line commit rationale citing dead-feature confirmation.
